characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A Birthday, Not a Break: For his tenth birthday, he receivesthe book. This would mark the start of the many adventures they are forced on. Future Badass: He will eventually fight his uncle Jack in a battle the will determine the fate of time and space. When this battle will take place is never stated. Heterosexual Life-Partners: With Sam and Fred. The Leader: Of the titular team. Ordinary High-School Student: Or elementary school student. The books have labeled Joe as an "ordinary kid". Turns out he may not be as ordinary as he thinks.

characters.name: Joe
characters.description: A Birthday, Not a Break: For his tenth birthday, he receivesthe book. This would mark the start of the many adventures they are forced on. Future Badass: He will eventually fight his uncle Jack in a battle the will determine the fate of time and space. When this battle will take place is never stated. Heterosexual Life-Partners: With Sam and Fred. The Leader: Of the titular team. Ordinary High-School Student: Or elementary school student. The books have labeled Joe as an "ordinary kid". Turns out he may not be as ordinary as he thinks.

characters.name: Sam
characters.description: Ambiguously Jewish: His first name Samuel is of Hebrew origin and he has many physical Jewish traits, such as having dark hair and olive skin. He also has many stereotypical characteristics such asbeing nerdy, having too many allergies, and speaking in a very nasally voice. Heterosexual Life-Partners: With Joe and Fred. Masculine Girl, Feminine Boy: He's the feminine boy to Samantha's masculine girl. Red Oni: To Fred's blue. The Smart Guy: The nerd among the main trio and the smartest one to boot.

characters.name: Fred
characters.description: Book Dumb: The dullest of the boys but is competent enough to get them out of trouble (though he very often gets them into it). Heterosexual Life-Partners: With Sam and Joe. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: For all his teasing and greed, he really does care about Joe and Sam and would do anything to help them. The Lancer: He's Joe's second-in-command sidekick of the trio. Not Me This Time: After the book sends them to Antartica, Joe and Sam blame him despite the fact he hadn't typed anything. Turns out it was Mad Jack who sent them there.

characters.name: Jodie
characters.description: The Ace: She's even more experienced with the book compared to Joe and is revealed to be a higher rank compared to him. Cool Big Sis: While not exactly sisters, she is this for Anna. Heterosexual Life-Partners: With Samantha and Freddi. The Leader: Of the titular trio in 2105. Pet the Dog: In the final episode, she goes back to 2005 so she can tutor her great aunt Anna with her math homework.

characters.name: Samantha
characters.description: Girlish Pigtails: Has six of them, three on each side of her head. Masculine Girl, Feminine Boy: She's the masculine girl to Sam's feminine boy. Town Girls: She's the butch to Jodie's feminine and Freddi's neither. Trademark Favorite Food: Marshmallows. She's also fond of onion rings.

characters.name: Freddi
characters.description: Catchphrase: "Stop or you'll disrupt the Time Space Continuum." Lovable Coward: Poor girl is afraid of nearly everything. Nice Girl: Out of all of the members of the group. she's probably the nicest. For one thing, she's the only member of the group whose NEVER insulted anyone. Only Sane Woman: She is ultimately described as this. Town Girls: She's the neither to Samantha's butch and Jodie's feminine.

characters.name: Anna
characters.description: Alliterative Name:AnnaArthur. Big Sister Worship: While not exactly a sister, she really idolizes Jodie and wishes she was her sister and not great grand niece. Sixth Ranger: Anna is a frequent companion of the trios, despite not technically being part of either trio herself. Tagalong Kid: Despite being younger than the rest of the cast, she often accompanies them on their adventures.

characters.name: Joe the Magnificent
characters.description: Big Good: The original owner of The Book and the primary ally of the trios that they rely on for help. One-Steve Limit: He shares the same name as Joe, which is intentional since Joe was named after him. The Pen Is Mightier: He possesses a magic, sentient pen as his primary weapon. It is aSwiss-Army Weaponcapable of unlocking any lock, generating aLaser Blade, and speaks its mind by writing out text.

characters.name: Mad Jack
characters.description: Big Bad: The main villain of the show, who is always out to steal The Book. Cane Fu: His weapon of choice is a cane with an hour glass on top. It is also aSwiss-Army Weaponwith a variety of functions such as time travelling, hacking, materializing/teleporting in objects, and generating aLaser Blade.
